Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike The Entirety Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-07-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS EDWARD OLSHANETSKY and BUSINESS INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT, INC.'S Motion To Strike The Entirety Of Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint and Dismiss Action is Denied. Although Plaintiff did not file her amended complaint within 10 days, it serves judicial economy to allow the amended complaint to stand. If the complaint were stricken, Plaintiff could file a motion for leave to file an amended complaint, which would almost certainly be granted given California's liberal policy towards amendments and the fact that the trial date has not been set. Defendants have not shown any prejudice from the minor delay in the filing of the amended complaint. Additionally, there is no basis to dismiss the complaint, as the demurrer was only sustained as to one cause of action. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
